developer.comment: Built and maintained by Google (google-gemini org).

status.comment: Active as of 2026-06-02, but Google announced (May 19, 2026) it will stop serving requests for free users, AI Pro/Ultra subscribers, and individual Code Assist users on June 18, 2026; enterprise customers retain it. Apache-2.0 repo stays public. A closed-source successor (Antigravity CLI) was introduced.

license.comment: Agent itself is Apache-2.0 open source; the hosted service is freemium (free tier plus paid plans).

specialization.comment: Broad coding/agentic terminal tool; not optimized for a single niche.

interface.comment: Terminal-first command-line agent. (A separate GitHub Action exists for CI, but the product itself is a CLI.)

host-ides.comment: Runs in the terminal; not an IDE plugin (the related Gemini Code Assist IDE integration is a separate product).

default-model-provider.comment: Defaults to Google's Gemini models (e.g. Gemini 2.5 Pro / latest).

model-choice.comment: User can select among Gemini models via the -m flag (e.g. gemini-2.5-flash vs 2.5-pro). Choice is within Google's model family only; non-Google providers are not natively supported.

bring-your-own-key.comment: Supports a personal Gemini API key or Vertex AI credentials in addition to OAuth sign-in.

local-model-support.comment: No official local-model support (no native Ollama/LM Studio/vLLM). Only Google-hosted Gemini models; local use relies on community forks or unofficial base-URL redirection. Checked 2026-06-02.

multi-file-editing.comment: Analyzes and edits across an entire repository in one task.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Agentic loop chains edit/run/fix steps toward a goal (subject to its permission gating).

terminal-command-execution.comment: Runs shell commands as part of its loop, with sandbox/trusted-folder controls.

mcp-support.comment: Documented MCP server integration; /mcp command lists configured servers.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Subagents defined via markdown + YAML frontmatter in .gemini/agents; built-in research subagents (codebase_investigator, cli_help) run in Plan Mode.

codebase-indexing.comment: Reads files on demand into context (large context window) rather than building a persistent semantic/embeddings index. No documented index store. Checked 2026-06-02.

planning-mode.comment: Documented Plan Mode (read-only plan-then-execute), expanded in 2026 with built-in research subagents.

skills.comment: Supports reusable custom slash commands and packaged subagents loaded on demand, beyond the GEMINI.md instruction file.

multimodal-input.comment: Accepts image input (images, PDFs, sketches) per the repo feature list.

browser-computer-use.comment: Has web search/fetch (grounding) tools but does not drive a real browser/GUI to act and verify its own changes. Checked 2026-06-02.

operating-mode.comment: Conversational REPL with per-action approval; can run multi-step tasks autonomously. No inline tab completion and no detached cloud/async dispatch.

inline-completion.comment: Terminal chat agent; no as-you-type tab autocomplete (that is the separate Gemini Code Assist IDE product).

background-async-execution.comment: Runs interactively in the foreground terminal session; not a fire-and-forget cloud/background dispatcher. (A non-interactive scripted mode exists but requires the session to run.)

persistent-memory.comment: Hierarchical project memory from GEMINI.md files loaded across sessions; /memory command manages it.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: /restore reverts project files to the state just before a tool was executed; conversation checkpointing also supported.

git-integration.comment: Can run git commands and is git-aware via its shell tool and GitHub Action integration.

pr-creation.comment: Via the official GitHub Action / GitHub integration it can open and update pull requests; the CLI itself can also drive git/gh.

hooks-extensibility.comment: Extensions (TypeScript modules adding custom tools/commands), custom slash commands, and MCP servers.

permission-model.comment: Prompts for approval before tool/command execution; offers sandbox and trusted-folder controls; auto-accept (YOLO) and policy settings make it configurable.

self-host-on-prem.comment: The CLI binary is local but depends on Google's hosted Gemini/Vertex backend; no on-prem/self-hosted model deployment. Checked 2026-06-02.

privacy-mode.comment: With Gemini Code Assist Standard/Enterprise, prompts and code are not collected or used to train models. The free individual tier may use data for training, so the zero-training guarantee is tier-dependent.

enterprise-controls.comment: Via Gemini Code Assist Enterprise on Google Cloud: SSO and RBAC through Cloud IAM, Cloud Audit Logs, and VPC Service Controls.

pricing-model.comment: Free tier (OAuth/free Code Assist), fixed-price subscriptions (Google AI Pro, Code Assist Standard/Enterprise), and pay-as-you-go via Gemini API / Vertex AI. Free individual tier is slated to stop June 18, 2026. Checked 2026-06-02.

free-tier.comment: Generous free tier (OAuth login: 60 req/min, 1,000 req/day) covering individual use. Note: Google announced this free tier ends June 18, 2026.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Genuine free tier covers core coding use (1,000 req/day). Cheapest paid plan is Google AI Pro at ~$19.99/mo; Code Assist Standard ~$22.80/user/mo. Free tier scheduled to end 2026-06-18.

maturity.comment: Polished, widely deployed GA tool with thousands of contributions, but rated 4 (not 5) given active flux and the May 2026 announced transition off the free/individual tier. Checked 2026-06-02.

community-size.comment: Google-backed with rapid, broad adoption and 6,000+ merged community PRs.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: Extensions, custom commands, and MCP server ecosystem; numerous community extensions and forks.
